name: dev-kernel
description: The invariant kernel of dev-factory — the typed contracts and deterministic computation a 'dark factory' agentic system runs on (it advances a knowledge lattice via an autonomous loop over a coordination corpus of work tickets). dev-kernel ships the 11 load-bearing schemas (cell · ticket · ledger-entry · activity · dispatch-policy/execution-plan · budget · lattice · roadmap · kit · adapter · naming), the two state machines (the eight-state cell maturity machine, vendored byte-identical from harness-forge, + the ticket lifecycle machine), the compass (deterministic ticket selection), execution-plan assembly, the validation path, the trust-trajectory + mechanical-demotion logic, the tamper-evident hash-chained ledger, FOUR protective gate scripts (gate-signal · gate-verifier · gate-ledger · gate-naming) + TWO lifecycle transition predicates (gate-ticket-ready · gate-dispatch), the kit-conform boundary gate, a read-only factory-query MCP, and a 12-agent roster across 8 compound skills. The reconciliation it defines: ticket `done` ⟺ the target cell advances through the SAME gate-signal — the board cannot disagree with the lattice. Computation routes to code; the model's judgment lives inside a cell. IMPORTANT — dev-kernel is the SUBSTRATE, not the running system: the protective gates BLOCK only once consent-wired into a worker loop, and the autonomous loop, the live operational state (the SQLite index the MCP reads), and the trust-tier enforcement are the separate **dev-server** app's. Installed alone it provides the contracts, the selftested kernel scripts, and the read perimeter; the dark factory runs when dev-server drives it. Vendors harness-forge's lattice/validation kernel via a drift-gated sync, never a cross-plugin import.
